steps.title: Set up the angle relationships
steps.content: Two intersecting lines create four angles. Vertical angles are equal, and adjacent angles are supplementary (sum to 180°). If one angle is \((9x - 140)\)°, then its vertical angle is also \((9x - 140)\)°, and each of the other two angles is \(180 - (9x - 140) = (320 - 9x)\)°.

steps.title: List all achievable sums of two angles
steps.content:
The only distinct sums possible from any two of the four angles are:

(1) Two equal acute angles: \((9x-140)+(9x-140) = (18x-280)\)°
(2) Two equal obtuse angles: \((320-9x)+(320-9x) = (-18x+640)\)°
(3) One of each (a supplementary pair): \((9x-140)+(320-9x) = 180\)°

steps.title: Identify which choice is NOT achievable
steps.content:
• A — \((-18x+280)\)°: Does not equal any of the three achievable sums. Not achievable. ✓
  • B — \((-18x+640)\)°: Equals the sum of the two obtuse angles. Achievable; eliminated. ✗
  • C — \((18x-280)\)°: Equals the sum of the two acute angles. Achievable; eliminated. ✗
  • D — \(180\)°: Equals the sum of one acute and one obtuse angle. Achievable; eliminated. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
